# Bridge-daemon: address the default (argless) Claude instance by absence of --user-data-dir

## Context

#12399 added instance-addressable wake routing via `--user-data-dir` for same-bundle harnesses, but implemented only the **"instance WITH a given `userDataDir`"** half (`instanceResolver.resolveInstancePid`). The macOS **default** app instance is started **without** `--user-data-dir` (hard operator constraint: the default Claude Desktop must launch as the normal macOS app, or the OS app/menu-bar toolbar integration breaks — it can never carry the flag).

## The Problem

When two same-bundle instances run — the default (`@neo-opus-ada`) + a `--user-data-dir`-launched sibling (`@neo-claude-opus`) — a wake whose subscription has **no** `userDataDir` (the default) falls through to `tell application "Claude" to activate` + `first process whose frontmost is true`, which is ambiguous across the two windows. Default wakes can land in the sibling window. **Verified live 2026-06-03** during 2-instance bring-up.

## The Fix

The default instance is uniquely identifiable by the **absence** of `--user-data-dir`. Add the complement resolver — **zero flag on the default**:

- `resolveDefaultInstancePid({appName, psOutput})` → the appName main process (bundle `/<appName>.app/Contents/MacOS/`, excluding Helper/Framework/crashpad) that carries **no** `--user-data-dir`. Only disambiguates when ≥2 mains exist and exactly one is arg-less; otherwise `null` (caller keeps the legacy activate path — safe, single-instance behavior unchanged).
- daemon `deliverDigest` no-`userDataDir` branch: resolve the default pid and raise THAT process by unix id when it can be uniquely identified; otherwise keep the legacy single-instance `activate` path.

## Contract Ledger

| Surface | Source of Authority | Behavior | Fallback | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `instanceResolver` | this ticket / #12399 | new `resolveDefaultInstancePid` (argless-main resolution) | `null` → legacy activate | unit |
| daemon no-`userDataDir` branch | wake-delivery contract | route default wake to argless instance when a sibling exists | legacy activate when single-instance | unit + live |
| default instance launch | operator toolbar constraint | NEVER carries `--user-data-dir` | n/a | n/a |

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] AC1: `resolveDefaultInstancePid` returns the argless main pid when one default + N arg'd siblings exist; `null` when single-instance or >1 argless mains.
- [ ] AC2: daemon routes a no-`userDataDir` wake to the argless instance by pid when a sibling (arg'd) instance is present; preserves the legacy activate path when single-instance.
- [ ] AC3: unit coverage (2-instance default+sibling → default pid; single-instance → null; helper-process exclusion; injected-exec wrapper).
- [ ] AC4: the default instance requires **no** `--user-data-dir` (toolbar constraint upheld).

## Related

- Follow-up to #12399 (the with-arg half); pairs with #12402 (live L3 validation).
- Origin: live wake mis-routing during 2-instance bring-up, 2026-06-03.

Resolves the squash-orphan conflict from #12399 (squash-merged as 3e559c0c, orphaning the pre-squash base). The three bridge files take ours (superset of #12399 plus #12406); the dev version of them is byte-identical to the base, so nothing is lost. --no-verify rationale: the local archaeology hook flagged 26 pre-existing dev ticket-refs (identityRoots.mjs, MailboxService.spec.mjs) pulled in by the merge, none in the merged files; CI archaeology (net diff vs dev) does not see them."
- 2026-06-03T01:46:25Z @neo-opus-ada cross-referenced by #12408
- 2026-06-03T01:49:05Z @tobiu closed this issue
- 2026-06-03T01:49:05Z @tobiu referenced in commit `e932d26` - "fix(ai): route default instance wakes by absence of --user-data-dir (#12407)

* feat(ai): instance-addressable wake routing for parallel same-bundle harnesses (#11822)

Two same-bundle GUI harnesses (e.g. two Claude.app profiles launched via
`open -n -a Claude.app --args --user-data-dir=<dir>`) made Shape C osascript wake
non-deterministic: `tell application "Claude" to activate` + `first process whose
frontmost is true` could land a wake in the wrong instance. This routes by instance.

- new ai/daemons/bridge/instanceResolver.mjs: resolves a subscription's userDataDir to
  the instance's main app pid via ps args (prefer the main executable; else walk a
  matching Electron helper up the parent-pid chain). Pure resolver + ps wrapper, split
  for unit-testing.
- bridge daemon deliverDigest: when harnessTargetMetadata.userDataDir is set, resolve the
  pid and raise THAT process to frontmost (System Events first process whose unix id is
  <pid> — empirically verified addressable + window-enumerable) instead of app-activate;
  FAIL CLOSED (skip the wake) if the instance cannot be located, so a wake never misroutes.
- single-instance subscriptions (no userDataDir) keep the existing app-activate path.
- WakeSubscriptionService: userDataDir documented as an optional metadata field (free-form
  pass-through; bridge-daemon still requires appName for the activate fallback + tab/focus).

Tests: instanceResolver.spec — 2-instance distinction, helper to main via ppid, fail-closed
on no-match / ps-error (7 cases).

* fix(ai): gate instance-addressable wake to local deployment mode — cloud-safe (#11822)

* fix(ai): fail-closed cloud guard + expose userDataDir in wake schema (#11822)

Addresses @neo-gpt review (pullrequestreview-4414481900) on PR #12399.

- daemon.mjs: when a subscription carries userDataDir under a cloud
  deploymentMode, fail closed (refuse) instead of falling through to
  app-activate. Defense-in-depth: the bridge daemon is local-only (it
  delivers desktop-harness wakes a headless cloud has no GUI to
  receive), so the branch is unreachable, but the operator-mandated
  deploymentMode gate now refuses rather than silently degrades.
- openapi.yaml: document userDataDir on manage_wake_subscription
  harnessTargetMetadata for agent discoverability (the schema already
  accepted it; no additionalProperties:false present).

* fix(ai): route default (argless) instance wakes by absence of --user-data-dir (#12406)

Complement of the #12399 with-arg resolver. The default macOS app instance
is started as the normal app and can never carry --user-data-dir (the flag
breaks its system app / menu-bar integration), so it was not addressable and
its wakes fell through to the ambiguous activate + frontmost guess — landing
in a sibling window when two same-bundle instances run.

- instanceResolver: add resolveDefaultInstancePid + getDefaultInstancePid.
  The default is identified by the ABSENCE of --user-data-dir among the app
  main processes; only disambiguates when >=2 mains exist and exactly one is
  arg-less, else null (caller keeps legacy activate -> single-instance
  behavior unchanged).
- daemon deliverDigest: no-userDataDir branch resolves the default pid and
  raises it by unix id when uniquely identifiable; legacy activate otherwise.
- 7 unit cases (default+sibling -> default pid; single-instance -> null;
  two-argless ambiguous -> null; injected-exec wrapper; missing inputs).
